description: Runneth gets to know each person it works with — their goals, preferences, and working style — and adapts to them over time. Standalone: resolves identity from Slack ID or Motion email and scaffolds each person's home base on first message. No permissions setup required.

requires.runtime: any Runneth sandbox; jq must be installed

customize.description: Path to the blank template used when creating a new team member file.
Path to the one-pager template used when creating a new conversation file.

changelog.notes: Standalone rebuild. Drops add-roles-permissions dependency. Ships its own lightweight slack-whoami.sh and a Neon-first motion-whoami.sh resolver (with motion-whoami-neon.py helper) that queries the Neon agent_conversation table for the speaker email and falls back to the local SQLite snapshot when Neon is unreachable plus an empty organization-map.json (renamed from workspace-map.json). On first message, auto-scaffolds each person's home base at /agent/brain/members/<handle>/ with a stub <handle>.md and a brain/ subfolder. Removes the Phase 1 pre-flight from behavior-snippet.md that was leaking the post-install intro quote into user.md (PDEC-7817). If add-roles-permissions is installed later, its stricter resolvers overwrite these and add scope rules on top of the same map file.
Added post-install intro. After install completes, Runneth surfaces a short 'what just opened up + try this now' message sourced from post-install-intro.md.

Now requires add-permissions. Identity resolution delegates to permissions resolvers (slack-whoami.sh / motion-whoami.sh) instead of maintaining its own user-map. Team files now live inside each member's home base. Slack surface now supported. user-map.json removed.
Initial release. Email-based user identification, team file per person with template, conversation one-pager system.

post-install.name: Introduce the new capability
post-install.description: Read post-install-intro.md from this use case folder. After all prior install and post-install steps complete, surface the file's sections to the user in chat as the closing message of the install turn: '## What just opened up' as prose, '## Try this now' as a numbered prompt list, '## Compounds with' only when present and at least one named use case is installed in the sandbox. Resolve any {{TOKEN}} placeholders using the same customize map applied during install.
